security.csp: default-src 'self' tauri:; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-eval' tauri:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: blob: https://*.cesium.com https://*.ion.cesium.com https://*.openstreetmap.org https://tile.openstreetmap.org https://*.arcgisonline.com https://server.arcgisonline.com; connect-src 'self' tauri: ipc: https://*.cesium.com https://*.ion.cesium.com https://*.openstreetmap.org https://tile.openstreetmap.org https://overpass-api.de https://*.arcgisonline.com https://server.arcgisonline.com; worker-src 'self' blob:; child-src 'self' blob:; font-src 'self' data:

bundle.shortDescription: Multi-hazard disaster simulator: asteroid impacts, nuclear detonations, earthquakes, landslides, tsunamis
bundle.longDescription: Cataclysm is a scientifically grounded 3D-globe desktop application for simulating catastrophic events and their effects. It models asteroid entry, airburst, cratering, thermal and blast effects (Holsapple, Collins et al.); nuclear weapon fireball, overpressure, thermal, radiation, EMP, fallout, and casualties (Glasstone-Dolan, NWFAQ); seismic and landslide sources; and the tsunamis these events generate (Ward & Asphaug, Le Méhauté, Fritz-Hager, Synolakis). Historical presets span Chicxulub, Tunguska, Chelyabinsk, Hiroshima, Tsar Bomba, Tōhoku 2011, and Lituya Bay 1958.
